skill.name: when-chaining-agent-pipelines-use-stream-chain
skill.description: Design, connect, run, monitor, and validate chained agent pipelines that pass outputs between stages.
skill.summary: Orchestrate sequential and parallel agent pipelines with streaming data flow.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many weak cryptography alerts, but review found no cryptographic code or hashing implementation in the scanned files. The confirmed risk is extensive shell-command guidance using npx claude-flow@alpha, a legitimate workflow pattern that still executes an unpinned external package.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned External CLI Execution Through npx
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to run npx claude-flow@alpha commands in several workflow examples. This appears aligned with the skill purpose, but it executes an external community package selected by a moving alpha tag, so package behavior can change without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command pattern is directly present across the documented scripts. Confidence is not higher because the commands are consistent with the stated workflow purpose and show no data exfiltration or destructive intent.

low_findings.title: Background Monitor Command May Persist
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: PROCESS.md starts a stream monitor with a trailing ampersand. This can leave a background process running after the main workflow completes, which is operationally risky but not malicious by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The background execution marker is clear, but impact depends on the user's shell session and the behavior of the claude-flow monitor command.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm findings, but review found no cryptographic APIs, hashing implementation, or encryption logic. The flagged lines describe pipeline design, workflow phases, validation steps, and diagram nodes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain workflow terms and command examples, not cryptographic functions. No evidence found for weak cryptography in the scanned files.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill includes executable shell examples that call npx claude-flow@alpha for pipeline setup, execution, monitoring, and validation. Users should review the package source and pin trusted versions before running these commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and repeated. They are risky because npx can download and run package code, but the content does not show malicious arguments.

content.value_statement: Multi-agent workflows can fail when outputs, dependencies, and validation steps are unclear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured process for designing and operating chained agent pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Designs sequential, parallel, and hybrid agent pipeline patterns.
Defines data flow between agent stages using memory and stream channels.
Provides command examples for pipeline setup, execution, monitoring, and validation.
Documents performance targets for latency, throughput, errors, and data integrity.
Lists common pipeline issues with practical troubleshooting guidance.
limitations: Requires Claude Flow or compatible tooling before commands can run.
Uses example stage names and inputs that must be adapted to each project.
Does not implement pipeline runtime code by itself.
Command examples rely on an external alpha package that should be reviewed first.

use_cases.title: Build Research-to-Code Workflows
use_cases.description: Create a pipeline where research output feeds analysis, implementation, testing, and review stages.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Parallel Agent Work
use_cases.description: Split independent tasks across agents, then merge outputs at defined synchronization points.

use_cases.title: Monitor Streaming Agent Results
use_cases.description: Track throughput, latency, backpressure, and validation status across chained agent stages.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a sequential agent pipeline for my project. Include stages, inputs, outputs, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Connect Pipeline Stages
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define memory and stream channels between these agent stages: research, analysis, coding, testing, and review.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Parallel Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to convert my sequential agent workflow into a parallel or hybrid pipeline with clear merge points and monitoring metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Pipeline Reliability
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review an existing agent pipeline for stalls, data loss, high latency, validation gaps, and backpressure risks.

output_examples.input: Design a five-stage agent workflow for feature delivery.
output_examples.output: A staged pipeline plan with research, analysis, implementation, testing, and review phases, including inputs, outputs, and success criteria.

output_examples.input: Find why my chained agents stop after stage two.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist focused on channel connections, missing stage output, backpressure, and validation failures.

output_examples.input: Add monitoring to a parallel agent workflow.
output_examples.output: A monitoring plan that tracks throughput, latency, error rate, backpressure, and per-stage progress.

best_practices: Validate each stage output before passing it to the next stage.
Pin and review external CLI packages before running workflow commands.
Track latency, throughput, error rate, and data integrity for every pipeline.
anti_patterns: Running copied npx commands without reviewing the package source or version.
Passing unvalidated agent output directly into downstream execution steps.
Mixing sequential and parallel stages without clear merge and error rules.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps design and operate chained agent pipelines where each stage passes useful output to later stages.

faq.question: Does it run the pipeline itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and command examples for Claude Flow or compatible tooling.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when coordinating agent workflows.

faq.question: What pipeline styles are covered?
faq.answer: It covers sequential, parallel, and hybrid pipelines with memory-based and stream-based data passing.

faq.question: What should I review before running commands?
faq.answer: Review the external claude-flow package, pin trusted versions, and check inputs before executing any command.

faq.question: How does it handle failures?
faq.answer: It recommends validation, monitoring, backpressure checks, retries, and clear stage boundaries to reduce workflow failures.
